coredata.dc:title: Utilization of doped/undoped graphene quantum dots for ultrasensitive detection of duphaston, a SERS platform
coredata.prism:publicationName: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy
coredata.dc:creator: Mary, Y.Sheena
Mary, Y.Shyma
coredata.dc:description: Recently nanocluster based drug delivery systems have become the most skillful to study. Interaction mechanism of duphaston (DPH) over graphene (G), carboxyl substituted graphene (COG) and doped COG-X (X = O/S/N/B) were investigated. We studied different spectroscopic properties of adsorbed DPH with nanoclusters. To study effect adsorption of DPH with nanoclusters, the adsorption energies were measured. To track DPH, surface enhanced Raman scattering is used since it is an efficient approach to vibrational spectroscopy. The DPH detection was investigated using GQDs SERS property. For the adsorption of DPH with COG-B nanocluster maximum energy interaction is determined. DPH works on the electrophilic site of nanoclusters as donor of electrons and adsorbs. Charge transfer is higher for to COG-B nanocluster than for other nanoclusters. Variations in chemical descriptors are also noted to understand sensing property of DPH molecule-nanoclusters. The analysis of different properties demonstrates enhancement effect which makes it significant in detecting DPH in other products.
coredata.dcterms:subject: DFT
Duphaston
Nanoclusters
Graphene
Carboxyl

xocs:meta.xocs:srctitle: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: SERS mechanism of DPH over nanoclusters

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Optimized geometry of (a) G-DPH (b) COG-DPH (c) COG-O-DPH (d) COG-S-DPH (e) COG-N-DPH (f) COG-B-DPH.

head.ce:title: Utilization of doped/undoped graphene quantum dots for ultrasensitive detection of duphaston, a SERS platform

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Physics, Fatima Mata National College (Autonomous), Kollam, Kerala, India
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Department of Physics, Fatima Mata National College (Autonomous), Kollam, Kerala, India

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Recently nanocluster based drug delivery systems have become the most skillful to study. Interaction mechanism of duphaston (DPH) over graphene (G), carboxyl substituted graphene (COG) and doped COG-X (X = O/S/N/B) were investigated. We studied different spectroscopic properties of adsorbed DPH with nanoclusters. To study effect adsorption of DPH with nanoclusters, the adsorption energies were measured. To track DPH, surface enhanced Raman scattering is used since it is an efficient approach to vibrational spectroscopy. The DPH detection was investigated using GQDs SERS property. For the adsorption of DPH with COG-B nanocluster maximum energy interaction is determined. DPH works on the electrophilic site of nanoclusters as donor of electrons and adsorbs. Charge transfer is higher for to COG-B nanocluster than for other nanoclusters. Variations in chemical descriptors are also noted to understand sensing property of DPH molecule-nanoclusters. The analysis of different properties demonstrates enhancement effect which makes it significant in detecting DPH in other products.

ce:section.ce:para: Dydrogesterone (Duphaston) (DPH) is a selective agonist of the progesterone receptor, with greater oral bioavailability [1,2]. The clear potency of oral dydrogesterone at low dosage decreases certain side effects and the probability of impaired liver function [3,4]. Steroids are commonly used substances that are responsible for regulating certain life-sustaining processes in living organisms. The testosterone and progesterone are made from a carbon network of androstane [5]. Mahmood et al. documented the development of a precise, reliable and easy estimation technique for analysis of betamethasone and tazarotene [6]. Estrogen and progesterone are two prime female reproductive hormones, have effects on multiple organs beyond the reproductive system. Steroidal hormones progesterone and testosterone have a crucial role in breast and prostate cancers and Jabeen et al. published docking analysis of progesterone and testosterone derivatives [7]. Ferraboschi et al. reported the spectroscopic and crystallographic analysis of an orally active progestin [8]. Phenanthren-3-one derivatives fall into the flucocorticoid groups having biological properties and is used to treat corticosteroid responsive dermatoses [9]. In environmental research, progestins attract considerable interest [10]. Dydrogesterone is used for gynacological disorders, hormone replacement therapy and persistent miscarriage [11–14]. Landge et al. reported the design of a triazole derivative of phenanthrene as a dual fluoride and copper recognition chemo sensor [15]. Graphene a two dimensional carbon atom layer exhibits remarkable Raman scattering properties because of good adsorptiveness and other chemical properties [16,17]. Graphene combination with metals improves graphene's Raman enhancement activity [18]. The study of graphene and its different forms indicate the potential role of it in SERS applications as a sensor [19–23]. GQDs may provide high SERS signals for identifying target molecules due to their effective photo electronic properties [24,25]. Anitha et al. reported the theoretical study of methane adsorption on graphene and faulty graphene [26]. Adsorption of molecules on graphene nanoclusters is a useful technique for the developing of nanosensor technology [27,28]. The graphene sheet's integrated electronic structure can be changed by putting in-plane defects in it [29–31]. DFT study of gas molecules adsorbed GDSs was reported by Montejo-Alvaro et al. [32]. The use of doped graphene for the improvement and detection of molecules by Raman signal has been confirmed [33,34]. Coronene is the smallest molecule, with properties that are closely mimic those of graphene as shown in literature [35–37]. In recent years, may improvements in various fields of science including small particles and nanostructured materials have been obtained [38–47]. In this article, investigation of spectroscopic properties in connection with adsorption of DPH over graphene (G), carboxyl group edge functionalized graphene (COG) and COG-X doped with X (X = O/S/N/B) atoms for its detection are reported.

To model the detection of DPH on GQD doped clusters, we initially carried out independent structure optimization of DPH (Fig. 1 ) and pristine undoped/doped GQDs. Surface-on model was adopted for the interaction of DPH on G/COG/COG-X. To investigate interaction of DPH molecule with nanoclusters wB97xD/6-31G* level of theory was applied using Gaussian09 [48] and Gaussview was used for visualization purpose [49]. The adsorption energy (Ead) of DPH over clusters is calculated using the method given by Al-Otaibi et al. [50–53] and negative value means stable complex of adsorption.

Before and after the adsorption of DPH molecule, the structural properties of graphene (G)/carboxyl functionalized graphene (COG)/oxygen, sulphur, nitrogen, boron doped COG (G/COG/COG-X(X = O, S, N,B)) were analyzed. Fig. S1 shows the optimized GQDs, graphene (G) and carboxy functionalized graphene (COG) structures. Option of C atom to replace X(X = O, S, N, B) was selectively made to avoid distortion and to retain GQDs functionalized edges. The hexagonal ring of carbon atoms (10, 7, 3, 4, 8, and 9) are suitable for best fitting for replacing atoms because of same environmental structure and coordination number of one and we select atom 8 for replacement. The size of COG and COG-X are 1.35 to 1.37 nm (for G 0.96 nm) which is enough for duphaston (DPH) adsorption and a single DPH molecule was placed of the nanocluster unit of GQD.

We explored FMOs (Fig. S3) of DPH over [G/COG/COG-X(X = O, S, N, B)] to study binding affinity between DPH and [G/COG/COG-X(X = O, S, N, B)]. In G-DPH, COG-O/S-DPH, HOMO is over the nanocluster and LUMO is over DPH while for others delocalization is over the nanocluster [59]. The chemical descriptors are given in Table S1. HOMO-LUMO's red and green colours, respectively, indicate positive and negative orbitals. HOMO-LUMO gap of DPH over GQD is maximum for DPH-G and minimum for DPH-B-COG. There are changes in Eg of DPH over all GQDs which show charge transfer. To understanding relation between the molecule's chemical behaviour, we measured and presented in Fig. S4 the molecular electrostatic potential DPH with nanoclusters. Red portions are electrophilic with more electrons according to the standards and blue gives nucleophilic activity with deficiency of electrons [61,62]. Red gives negative potential where positively charges are strongly bound and vice versa for blue. This gives strong interaction between DPH and boron doped GQD with a high adsorption energy for B-doped system.

Provided that the distribution of charges has a major impact on vibrational properties, distribution of charge of all complexes is investigated by measuring Mulliken population analysis. Here the carbon bears both positive and negative charges while the charges for hydrogen are only positive. For DPH adsorbed on nanoclusters of all systems, the oxygen atoms of DPH receive more charges (Table S2). All the oxygen atoms in the carboxylic group also undergo an enhancement in charge value except for N-doped system after and before adsorption. Out of the doped atoms (O/S/N/B), only the charge of boron atom changes from positive value to negative value after adsorption of DPH with a reduction in value while all other doped atoms (O/S/N) keep the polarity of charge with an enhancement of the value. High charge transfer in DPH over B-GQD is due to high energy adsorption. The Mulliken charge analysis of structures confirms the effects of chemical enhancement. The UV visible absorption of DPH (284.5) is blue shifted in all nanoclusters with high absorption for all B/N doped clusters (Fig. S5).

Molecular nonlinear optical activity materials are currently under study and of considerable interest as they involve new technological features due to evolving utility of electronic devices. First-order hyperpolarizability (×10−30 esu) of all molecules of DPH with nanoclusters except with graphene and GCCOH are greater than that of DPH. The first order hyperpolarizability of similar derivatives are, 8.04 (×10−30 esu [63], 12.28 × 10−30 esu [64], and 6.51 × 10−30 esu [65]). There is very large enhancement for the second hyperpolarizability of all complexes showing high nonlinear optical properties for the complexes (Table S3). The polarizability also shows enhancement in comparison with that of pristine DPH [66,67].

ce:section.ce:section-title: SERS mechanism of DPH over nanoclusters
ce:section.ce:para: SERS enhancement properties of DPH over nanoclusters have been studied using DFT calculations to test DPH detection. Fig. S6 shows Raman and SERS spectra of DPH and DPH-GQDs. For all nanoclusters, there is an enhancement for the CO stretching modes of DPH (1704 and 1658 cm−1) with a red shift except for COG-S-DPH which has a blue shift (Table S4). Maximum enhancement is seen for COG-B-DPH with an enhancement factor 1078 for CO mode. The CH3 stretching mode at 3005 and 2989 cm−1 of DPH are enhanced for all nanoclusters except for COG-S-DPH. But the CH3 deformation at 1360 cm−1 undergoes an enhancement in all nanoclusters with maximum enhancement in G-DPH (1413) and minimum in COG-DPH (207). For all nanoclusters, the CH2 deformation at 1221 cm−1 undergoes a blue shift except for G-DPH. This indicates that it is possible to make a graphene-sensor for detection of DPH using SERS [50–53].

We have calculated the chemical enhancement for DPH over the nanoclusters and found that enhancement is observed for all nanoclusters. Because of the chemical interactions in the molecule, boron doped nanocluster has more adsorption energy and high charge transfer than that of pristine and other systems. The findings of the UV–vis spectra showed that the complex of the DPH-nanocluster shows a change toward higher wavenumbers. Compared with the absorption spectrum of pure DPH (284.5 nm), all the nanoclusters show a wavenumber blue shift and B doping contribute to a high blue shift (912 nm). A drug delivery system is expected to be capable of utilizing DPH drug over nano cluster. At adsorption of DPH, the band gap of each nanocluster is reduced significantly. Findings of the present work show that GQDs are suitable for DPH detection and DPH is prone to nanoclusters being present as a substrate.

Y.Sheena Mary: Conceptualization, Data curation, Formal analysis, Investigation, Writing - original draft, Writing - review & editing. Y.Shyma Mary: Data curation, Formal analysis, Investigation, Writing - original draft, Writing - review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.saa.2020.118865.
